title: Leave Letter to Professor
template:
Dear Professor,

I am {{name}}, a student of {{department}}, Year {{year}} at {{college}}. I am writing to respectfully request leave from [DATE] to [DATE] due to [REASON].

I assure you that I will complete all pending assignments and catch up on missed lectures upon my return.

Thank you for your understanding.

Yours sincerely,
{{name}}
{{department}}, Year {{year}}
preview: Formal leave letter template for requesting absence from college/university classes.

title: Assignment Extension Request
template:
Subject: Request for Assignment Extension – [SUBJECT NAME]

Dear Professor [PROFESSOR NAME],

I am {{name}} from {{department}}, Year {{year}} at {{college}}. I am writing to request a short extension for the [ASSIGNMENT NAME] due on [ORIGINAL DATE].

Due to [REASON], I was unable to complete the assignment to the best of my ability. I kindly request an extension until [NEW DATE].

I appreciate your consideration and will ensure timely submission.

Sincerely,
{{name}}
preview: Politely request an extension for an assignment deadline with a valid reason.

title: Permission Letter for Event Participation
template:
To,
The Head of Department,
{{department}},
{{college}}

Subject: Permission to Participate in [EVENT NAME]

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, {{name}}, student of {{department}} Year {{year}}, humbly request your permission to participate in [EVENT NAME] to be held at [VENUE] on [DATE].

This opportunity will help me gain practical exposure and represent our department positively.

I assure you that I will not miss any important academic duties.

Yours obediently,
{{name}}
{{year}}, {{department}}
preview: Request permission to attend or participate in an academic or extracurricular event.

title: AI Tutor for Difficult Concepts
template:
Act as a patient and knowledgeable tutor for a {{year}} year student studying {{department}}.

Explain the concept of [TOPIC] in a clear, step-by-step manner. Use simple language, real-world examples, and analogies relevant to a student at {{college}}.

After the explanation, provide 3 practice questions with answers to test understanding.
preview: Get a step-by-step explanation of any difficult academic topic with practice questions.

title: Personalized Study Plan Creator
template:
Create a detailed 4-week study plan for {{name}}, a {{year}} year {{department}} student at {{college}}.

Exam/Goal: [EXAM NAME or GOAL]
Available study hours per day: [HOURS]
Weak areas: [LIST WEAK TOPICS]
Strong areas: [LIST STRONG TOPICS]

The plan should include:
- Daily topic schedule
- Revision days
- Practice test days
- Tips for each week

Make it realistic and motivating.
preview: Generate a personalized weekly study schedule tailored to your exam and subjects.

title: Explain Like I'm Five (ELI5)
template:
Explain [COMPLEX TOPIC] as if I am a complete beginner with no prior knowledge.

Use:
- Very simple words
- A fun real-world analogy or story
- Short sentences
- A summary at the end

My background: I am a {{year}} year {{department}} student. Keep the explanation friendly and engaging.
preview: Break down any complex topic into super simple language anyone can understand.

title: Research Topic Ideas Generator
template:
I am {{name}}, a {{year}} year student of {{department}} at {{college}}.

Generate 10 unique and interesting research project or paper topics in the field of [SUBJECT AREA].

For each topic:
1. Topic title
2. Brief description (2-3 sentences)
3. Why it is relevant and impactful
4. Suggested research approach

Focus on topics that are practical, researchable, and suitable for an undergraduate/postgraduate level.
preview: Discover fresh and impactful research project ideas in your field of study.

title: Text Summarizer for Study Notes
template:
Summarize the following text into concise study notes for a {{department}} student.

Requirements:
- Bullet point format
- Highlight key terms in bold
- Keep it under 200 words
- Add a 1-line conclusion

Text to summarize:
[PASTE YOUR TEXT HERE]
preview: Convert long study material into concise bullet-point notes instantly.

title: Professional Resume Builder
template:
Create a professional resume for {{name}}, a final year {{department}} student from {{college}}.

Personal Details:
- Name: {{name}}
- Department: {{department}}
- College: {{college}}
- Skills: [LIST YOUR SKILLS]
- Internships/Projects: [DESCRIBE YOUR EXPERIENCE]
- Achievements: [LIST ACHIEVEMENTS]
- Certifications: [LIST CERTIFICATIONS]

Format the resume with:
1. Professional Summary
2. Education
3. Skills
4. Projects / Internships
5. Achievements
6. Certifications

Use a clean, ATS-friendly format.
preview: Build a complete, professional ATS-friendly resume using your academic and personal details.

title: Resume Improvement & Feedback
template:
Review and improve the following resume for {{name}}, a {{department}} graduate from {{college}}.

Analyze for:
1. ATS optimization
2. Clarity and impact of bullet points
3. Skills alignment with [TARGET JOB ROLE]
4. Missing sections
5. Professional tone and formatting

Provide:
- Specific suggestions for each section
- Improved versions of weak bullet points
- A score out of 10 for overall quality

Resume content:
[PASTE RESUME HERE]
preview: Get detailed feedback and improvement suggestions to strengthen your existing resume.

title: Interview Preparation Coach
template:
Act as an experienced interview coach preparing {{name}} for a [JOB ROLE] interview.

Background: {{name}} is a {{department}} graduate from {{college}}.

Provide:
1. Top 10 likely interview questions for [JOB ROLE]
2. Model answers tailored to {{name}}'s background
3. Tips for answering behavioral questions using STAR method
4. Common mistakes to avoid
5. 5 smart questions {{name}} should ask the interviewer

Make the preparation realistic and role-specific.
preview: Prepare for job interviews with role-specific questions, model answers, and coaching tips.

title: Internship Application Email
preview: Send a professional internship application email that stands out to recruiters.

title: Job Application Email
preview: Write a compelling job application email tailored to your target role and company.

title: Follow-Up Email After Interview
template:
Subject: Thank You – [JOB ROLE] Interview on [DATE]

Dear [INTERVIEWER NAME],

Thank you for taking the time to interview me for the [JOB ROLE] position at [COMPANY NAME] on [DATE]. It was a pleasure learning more about the role and the team.

Our conversation reinforced my enthusiasm for this opportunity. I am particularly excited about [SPECIFIC ASPECT DISCUSSED] and I am confident that my background in {{department}} from {{college}} makes me a strong fit.

Please let me know if you need any additional information. I look forward to hearing from you regarding the next steps.

Warm regards,
{{name}}
[PHONE] | [EMAIL]
preview: Send a professional thank-you follow-up email after your job or internship interview.

title: Medical Leave Letter
template:
To,
The Class Teacher / HOD,
{{department}} Department,
{{college}}

Subject: Application for Medical Leave

Respected Sir/Madam,

I, {{name}}, student of {{department}}, Year {{year}}, am writing to inform you that I am unable to attend classes from [START DATE] to [END DATE] due to [MEDICAL REASON / ILLNESS].

I have attached a medical certificate from [DOCTOR/HOSPITAL NAME] for your reference.

I kindly request you to grant me medical leave for the mentioned period. I will ensure that I cover all missed coursework upon my return.

Thank you for your kind consideration.

Yours sincerely,
{{name}}
{{year}} Year, {{department}}
preview: Apply for medical leave with a properly formatted letter and doctor's certificate reference.

title: HR Job Description Writer
template:
Write a comprehensive and attractive job description for the role of [JOB TITLE] at [COMPANY NAME].

Include:
1. Job Title and Department
2. About the Company (brief)
3. Role Overview (3-4 sentences)
4. Key Responsibilities (8-10 bullet points)
5. Required Qualifications
6. Preferred Qualifications
7. Skills Required
8. What We Offer (benefits, culture)
9. How to Apply

Tone: Professional, inclusive, and engaging.
Level: [JUNIOR / MID / SENIOR]
preview: Create a complete and compelling job description to attract the right candidates.

title: Employee Performance Review
template:
Write a professional performance review for [EMPLOYEE NAME] for the period [REVIEW PERIOD].

Employee Details:
- Role: [JOB ROLE]
- Department: [DEPARTMENT]
- Manager: [MANAGER NAME]

Evaluate the following:
1. Goal Achievement (rate 1-5 and comment)
2. Quality of Work
3. Teamwork & Collaboration
4. Communication Skills
5. Problem-Solving Ability
6. Areas for Improvement
7. Overall Rating
8. Goals for Next Period

Tone: Constructive, fair, and professional.
preview: Draft a structured and professional employee performance review or appraisal report.

title: Cold Email to Recruiter on LinkedIn
template:
Write a concise and professional cold outreach message to a recruiter on LinkedIn.

Sender: {{name}}, {{department}} graduate from {{college}}
Target Role: [DESIRED JOB ROLE]
Company: [COMPANY NAME]
Recruiter Name: [RECRUITER NAME]

The message should:
- Be under 150 words
- Mention a specific reason for reaching out
- Highlight one key strength
- Request a short call or connection
- Sound human and not generic

Tone: Friendly, professional, and confident.
preview: Craft a short, impactful LinkedIn cold message to reach out to recruiters professionally.

title: Project Report Introduction Writer
template:
Write a professional introduction section for a project report submitted by {{name}}, a {{year}} year {{department}} student at {{college}}.

Project Title: [PROJECT TITLE]
Project Domain: [DOMAIN / SUBJECT AREA]
Objective: [MAIN OBJECTIVE]
Scope: [WHAT THE PROJECT COVERS]

The introduction should:
- Start with a broad context of the topic
- Narrow down to the specific problem
- State the objective clearly
- Briefly mention the methodology
- End with the structure of the report

Word count: 300-400 words.
Tone: Academic and formal.
preview: Write a polished academic introduction section for your project report or thesis.

title: Scholarship Application Letter
template:
Write a compelling scholarship application letter for {{name}}, a {{year}} year {{department}} student at {{college}}.

Scholarship Name: [SCHOLARSHIP NAME]
Awarding Body: [ORGANIZATION]
Amount: [SCHOLARSHIP AMOUNT]

The letter should include:
1. Personal introduction and academic background
2. Financial need or merit (as applicable)
3. Career goals and aspirations in {{department}}
4. Why {{name}} deserves this scholarship
5. How the scholarship will help
6. Closing statement

Tone: Sincere, professional, and persuasive.
Word count: 400-500 words.
preview: Write a heartfelt and compelling scholarship application letter to maximize your chances.

title: Group Study Session Planner
template:
Plan an effective group study session for {{department}} students at {{college}}.

Group Size: [NUMBER OF STUDENTS]
Subject: [SUBJECT NAME]
Duration: [TOTAL HOURS]
Exam Date: [DATE]

Create a structured plan that includes:
1. Session schedule with time slots
2. Topic allocation per student
3. Teaching rotation (each student teaches one topic)
4. Break schedule
5. Quick quiz at the end
6. Rules for a productive session

Make it collaborative and engaging.
preview: Organize a productive group study session with a structured schedule and topic division.

title: GitHub README Writer
template:
Write a professional GitHub README.md file for a project by {{name}} from {{department}}, {{college}}.

Project Name: [PROJECT NAME]
Description: [WHAT THE PROJECT DOES]
Tech Stack: [LANGUAGES/FRAMEWORKS USED]
Features: [LIST KEY FEATURES]

The README should include:
1. Project title and badges
2. Description
3. Features list
4. Tech stack
5. Installation instructions
6. Usage guide
7. Screenshots section placeholder
8. Contributing guidelines
9. License
10. Contact / Author info

Make it visually structured using Markdown.
preview: Generate a complete and professional README.md file for your GitHub project.

title: Offer Letter Template (HR)
template:
Write a formal offer letter from [COMPANY NAME] to [CANDIDATE NAME] for the position of [JOB TITLE].

Details:
- Start Date: [DATE]
- Salary: [AMOUNT]
- Department: [DEPARTMENT]
- Reporting Manager: [MANAGER NAME]
- Location: [LOCATION]
- Employment Type: [FULL TIME / PART TIME / CONTRACT]

The letter should include:
1. Formal greeting and offer statement
2. Role and responsibilities summary
3. Compensation details
4. Benefits overview
5. Terms and conditions
6. Acceptance deadline
7. Closing and signature block

Tone: Professional, welcoming, and legally appropriate.
preview: Draft a formal and complete job offer letter for HR and recruitment purposes.

title: Debate Argument Builder
template:
Help {{name}}, a {{department}} student from {{college}}, prepare strong arguments for a debate.

Debate Topic: [TOPIC]
Side: [FOR / AGAINST]
Debate Format: [BRITISH / OXFORD / OPEN]

Provide:
1. Opening statement (60-90 seconds)
2. 3 strong main arguments with evidence/examples
3. Anticipated counter-arguments and rebuttals
4. Closing statement
5. Key statistics or facts to mention

Tone: Persuasive, logical, and confident.
Level: College/University competition.
preview: Build powerful debate arguments with opening statements, rebuttals, and closing lines.

title: Apology Letter to Professor
template:
Write a sincere apology letter from {{name}}, a {{year}} year {{department}} student at {{college}}, to Professor [PROFESSOR NAME].

Reason for Apology: [DESCRIBE THE INCIDENT]

The letter should:
1. Open with a respectful greeting
2. Clearly acknowledge the mistake
3. Express genuine remorse
4. Explain (not excuse) what happened
5. State how {{name}} will prevent it from happening again
6. Request forgiveness and continued guidance

Tone: Humble, respectful, and sincere.
Length: 150-200 words.
preview: Write a respectful and sincere apology letter to your professor for any academic misconduct.
